content:
You are playing Minesweeper on an Android screen.
Analyze the current grid-overlaid screenshot and use your tools to play the game.
If you win or lose the game, do not call any more tools and output a message explaining the final result.
Here is the screen state at this step. [Previous screenshot grid omitted to optimize context]

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"x":550,"reason":"Starting tap near the center of the board to open up some space.","y":1250}

name: tap
content: Successfully tapped coordinate (550, 1250).
Here is the screen state at this step. [Previous screenshot grid omitted to optimize context]

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"y":1350,"reason":"Cell (C2, R13) is safe because (C3, R11) is 1 and its only closed neighbor is (C2, R12) which must be a mine, satisfying (C3, R12) = 1, which makes (C2, R13) safe.","x":250}

name: tap
content: Successfully tapped coordinate (250, 1350).
Here is the screen state at this step. [Previous screenshot grid omitted to optimize context]

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"x":750,"y":930,"reason":"(C9, R12) must be a mine because it is the only closed neighbor of (C8, R12) which is 1. This means (C9, R10) is safe as it is a closed neighbor of (C9, R11) which is 1 and already has its mine at (C9, R12)."}

name: tap
content: Successfully tapped coordinate (750, 930).
Here is the screen state at this step. [Previous screenshot grid omitted to optimize context]

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"reason":"C8, R14 (750, 1350) is safe because C10, R13 (1) restricts its mine to {C9, R12, C10, R12}, and since C9, R13 (1) shares those same closed neighbors, its remaining closed neighbor C8, R14 must be safe.","x":750,"y":1350}

name: tap
content: Successfully tapped coordinate (750, 1350).
Here is the screen state at this step. [Previous screenshot grid omitted to optimize context]

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"reason":"(C9, R10) is safe because (C8, R10) is 1 and its only mine neighbor must be (C8, R9), which is the sole closed neighbor of (C7, R10) = 1.","x":860,"y":1050}

name: tap
content: Successfully tapped coordinate (860, 1050).
Here is the screen state at this step. [Previous screenshot grid omitted to optimize context]

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"x":50,"reason":"C1, R13 is safe because C2, R13 is 1 and its only mine neighbor must be C2, R14 (which is the sole closed neighbor of C3, R13 = 1).","y":1250}

name: tap
content: Successfully tapped coordinate (50, 1250).
Here is the screen state at this step. [Previous screenshot grid omitted to optimize context]

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"reason":"Since C3, R13 is 1 and its only covered neighbor is C2, R14, C2, R14 must be a mine. Since C2, R13 is 1 and already has its mine at C2, R14, its other covered neighbors C1, R12 and C1, R14 must be safe. Tap C1, R12.","y":1150,"x":50}

name: tap
content: Successfully tapped coordinate (50, 1150).
Here is the screen state at this step. [Previous screenshot grid omitted to optimize context]

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"y":1750,"reason":"C9, R18 is safe because C8, R18 is 1 and its mine is at C7, R19.","x":850}

name: tap
content: Successfully tapped coordinate (850, 1750).
Here is the screen state at this step. [Previous screenshot grid omitted to optimize context]

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"x":50,"reason":"C2, R12 is a mine because C1, R12 (1) only has C2, R12 as a covered neighbor. Thus C1, R11 (1) has its mine at C2, R12, which makes C1, R10 and C2, R10 safe. Let's tap C1, R10 first.","y":970}

name: tap
content: Successfully tapped coordinate (50, 970).
Here is the screen state at this step. [Previous screenshot grid omitted to optimize context]

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"reason":"C2, R10 is safe because C2, R12 is a mine (sole closed neighbor of C1, R12) which satisfies C1, R11.","y":950,"x":150}

name: tap
content: Successfully tapped coordinate (150, 950).
Here is the screen state at this step. [Previous screenshot grid omitted to optimize context]

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"reason":"C3, R11 is a mine (by C2, R11 = 2 and C1, R12 = 1 logic), which satisfies C3, R10 = 1. Therefore, C3, R9 is safe.","x":250,"y":850}

name: tap
content: Successfully tapped coordinate (250, 850).
Here is the screen state at this step. [Previous screenshot grid omitted to optimize context]

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"x":50,"y":1350,"reason":"C3, R13 is 1 and its only closed neighbor is C2, R14, so C2, R14 is a mine. Since C1, R13 is 1 and already has its mine at C2, R14, its other closed neighbor C1, R14 must be safe."}

name: tap
content: Successfully tapped coordinate (50, 1350).
Here is the screen state at this step. [Previous screenshot grid omitted to optimize context]

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"reason":"C2, R15 is safe because C1, R13 is 1 and its only closed neighbor is C2, R14 (which is therefore a mine), and C1, R14 (1) has closed neighbors C2, R14 and C2, R15, making C2, R15 safe.","x":150,"y":1450}

name: tap
content: Successfully tapped coordinate (150, 1450).

content.text: Here is the new screen state after executing your action.

function.name: tap
function.arguments: {"x":150,"reason":"C2, R1400-1500 is a mine because C2, R1300-1400 is 1 and its only closed neighbor is C2, R1400-1500. Thus, C1, R1400-1500 (1) already has its mine at C2, R1400-1500, meaning its other closed neighbor C2, R1500-1600 is safe.","y":1500}
